tool.name: crtsh_search
tool.description:
Search Certificate Transparency logs via crt.sh for subdomain discovery.
This is a PASSIVE reconnaissance tool - it queries crt.sh, NOT the target. Discovers subdomains by finding certificates issued for the domain.
Use this BEFORE active scanning to expand your attack surface.

parameter.name: domain
parameter.description: Target domain to search (e.g., "example.com"). Will extract root domain from URLs.

parameter.name: include_expired
parameter.description: Include expired certificates in results (default: false)

returns.description: Response containing: - 'success': Whether the search succeeded - 'subdomains': List of unique subdomains found - 'subdomain_count': Number of subdomains - 'certificates': List of certificate details - 'total_certs': Total certificates found

tool.examples:
# Find all subdomains for a target
example.com
# Include expired certificates for historical analysis
target.org true

tool.name: shodan_search
tool.description:
Search Shodan for exposed services, open ports, and vulnerabilities.
This is a PASSIVE reconnaissance tool - it queries Shodan's database, NOT the target directly. Requires SHODAN_API_KEY environment variable.
Use to find: - Exposed services and open ports - Technology versions (for CVE mapping) - Known vulnerabilities (CVEs) - Cloud infrastructure details

parameter.name: query
parameter.description:
Search query - can be: - IP address (e.g., "8.8.8.8") - Domain (e.g., "example.com") - Shodan dork (e.g., "hostname:example.com port:443")
Common dorks: - hostname:example.com - All IPs for domain - org:"Company Name" - IPs by organization - port:22 - SSH servers - product:nginx - Specific product - vuln:CVE-XXXX-XXXXX - Known vulnerabilities - ssl.cert.subject.cn:example.com - By SSL cert

parameter.name: search_type
parameter.description: Type of search: "host" (specific IP/domain lookup) or "search" (general dork search). Default: "host"

returns.description: Response containing: - 'success': Whether the search succeeded - 'results': List of found hosts/services with ports, products, versions - 'vulns': List of CVEs found (if any) - 'total': Total results in Shodan - 'hostnames': Associated hostnames - 'org': Organization name

tool.examples:
# Look up a specific target
example.com host
# Search for all nginx servers in an org
org:"Target Corp" product:nginx search
# Find systems with a known vulnerability
vuln:CVE-XXXX-XXXXX search

tool.name: whois_lookup
tool.description:
Perform WHOIS lookup for domain registration information.
This is a PASSIVE reconnaissance tool - it queries WHOIS servers, NOT the target directly.
Useful for: - Finding registrar and registration dates - Identifying name servers - Discovering related domains via registrant info - Timeline analysis (when was domain created?)

parameter.name: include_history
parameter.description: Try to get historical WHOIS data (requires premium API key)

returns.description: Response containing: - 'success': Whether the lookup succeeded - 'registrar': Domain registrar - 'creation_date': Domain creation date - 'expiration_date': Domain expiration date - 'name_servers': List of name servers - 'registrant': Registrant information (if available)

tool.name: dns_enum
tool.description:
Enumerate DNS records for a domain using multiple sources.
This is a PASSIVE reconnaissance tool - it queries DNS servers and public APIs, NOT the target directly.
Discovers: - A, AAAA, MX, NS, TXT, CNAME, SOA records - Subdomains from DNS records - Common subdomain prefixes (www, mail, api, dev, etc.)

parameter.name: record_types
parameter.description: List of record types to query. Default: ["A", "AAAA", "MX", "NS", "TXT", "CNAME", "SOA"]

returns.description: Response containing: - 'success': Whether enumeration succeeded - 'records': Dictionary of record type -> list of values - 'subdomains': Discovered subdomains from DNS - 'record_count': Total records found

tool.examples:
example.com
# Query specific record types
example.com ["A", "MX", "TXT"]

tool.name: github_dork
tool.description:
Search GitHub for leaked secrets, exposed configs, and sensitive data.
This is a PASSIVE reconnaissance tool - it queries GitHub's API, NOT the target directly. Set GITHUB_TOKEN for better rate limits.
Finds: - Hardcoded credentials and API keys - Internal URLs and endpoints - Configuration files with secrets - Database connection strings - AWS/GCP/Azure credentials

parameter.name: domain
parameter.description: Domain to search for in code (e.g., "example.com")

returns.description: Response containing: - 'success': Whether the search succeeded - 'results': List of potential exposures with repo, file, and URL - 'total_count': Total matching results - 'queries_run': Number of search queries executed

tool.notes: At least one of 'organization' or 'domain' must be provided. Set GITHUB_TOKEN environment variable for higher rate limits.
tool.examples:
# Search for secrets in a specific organization
targetcorp
# Search for domain mentions with specific keywords
target.com ["password", "api_key", "aws_secret"]
